summary: Sulfuric acid causes typical signs like blistering and ulceration but surprisingly leaves hair undamaged; coagulative necrosis occurs deep within tissues leading to extensive damage below skin surfaces while sparing superficial features including hairs. Therefore, option C stands out accurately describing an atypical feature specific only among listed conditions regarding its impact versus other chemicals mentioned concerning their effects post-exposure scenarios involving human anatomy exposed directly by corrosives like acids used maliciously causing severe injuries requiring immediate medical attention especially when targeting
